---
title: "Lecture 16: Arrow Functions & Callbacks"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "February 19, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---


## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 16]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Arrow Functions & Callbacks]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 4 | Unit II: JavaScript Functions & Events<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Write concise arrow functions
- Understand callback fundamentals
- Use callbacks with array methods
- Recognize synchronous flow with callbacks
- Practice cleaner modern JavaScript
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Arrow Basics"}

[Arrow Function Syntax]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4"}
const add = (a, b) => a + b;
const square = x => x * x;
const greet = () => "Hello";
const full = (first, last) => `${first} ${last}`;
```

Arrow functions are compact and readable for small logic.

---

## {data-menu-title="Regular vs Arrow"}

[Regular Function vs Arrow]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```javascript
function multiply(a, b) {
  return a * b;
}

const multiplyArrow = (a, b) => a * b;
```

Both work; arrow syntax is shorter in many cases.

---

## {data-menu-title="Callback Concept"}

[What Is a Callback?]{.slide-title}

<hr>

A callback is a function passed into another function.

```javascript
function greetUser(name, callback) {
  const msg = `Hello, ${name}`;
  callback(msg);
}

greetUser("Anu", message => console.log(message));
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Array Callbacks"}

[Array Methods with Callbacks]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4"}
const nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
const doubled = nums.map(n => n * 2);
const evens = nums.filter(n => n % 2 === 0);
const total = nums.reduce((acc, n) => acc + n, 0);
```

- `map`: transform
- `filter`: select
- `reduce`: combine

---

## {data-menu-title="forEach"}

[forEach with Arrow Function]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```javascript
const students = ["Asha", "Vikram", "Neha"];
students.forEach((name, index) => {
  console.log(index, name);
});
```

Useful for side effects like logging or UI updates.

---

## {data-menu-title="Callback Flow"}

[Callback Flow Pattern]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```javascript
function process(data, callback) {
  const result = data.trim().toUpperCase();
  callback(result);
}

process("  web tech  ", output => {
  console.log(output); // WEB TECH
});
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Common Mistakes"}

[Common Mistakes]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Forgetting to return value from block-body arrow function
- Passing `callback()` instead of `callback`
- Overusing nested callbacks without structure
- Using arrow functions when `this` from method context is needed
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Practice"}

[Practice Task]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Write a function `transformNumbers(arr, callback)` that:

1. accepts an array of numbers
2. applies callback to each number
3. returns transformed array

```javascript
const result = transformNumbers([2, 4, 6], x => x / 2);
```

---

## {#end-slide data-menu-title="End Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="page-center">
<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Questions?]{.white-text}
</div>
<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Next: Week 5 - DOM Manipulation
</div>
</div>
